title: reissantia indica var. orientalis
intro: Reissantia indica var. orientalis, also known as the 'Indian Root', is a type of plant native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Celastraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Reissantia indica var. orientalis include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and fever. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be made by boiling the root in water or mixing it with other herbs to create a medicinal concoction.
